transliteration:
suhṛn-mitrāry-udāsīna-madhyastha-dveṣya-bandhuṣu
sādhuṣv api ca pāpeṣu sama-buddhir viśiṣyate
literal_meaning: One whose intellect is the same toward well-wishers, friends, foes, neutrals, mediators, the hateful, relatives, the virtuous and even the sinful—such a person excels.
contextual_meaning: The yogi has moved beyond splitting the world into ‘good’ and ‘bad’ people; every figure is seen as a transient role in the same field of consciousness, removing the emotional charge that fuels reactivity.
psychological_state: Post-ambivalent object constancy: inner attachment to praise or blame dissolves, producing stable, non-reactive warmth.
trigger_scenario: A manager hears the same project idea from a trusted teammate and from a rival she dislikes; she responds with identical attention and respect, noticing no internal tug to side with the familiar face.
anchor_text: Meet every face as a passing scene in one calm awareness.
